content.text:
Context: `links init` — scaffold a new link page project
Objective: Implement the `links init` command. When run in an empty or new directory, it creates a links.yaml with sensible defaults and prints a helpful getting-started message. Should be safe to run — never overwrite an existing links.yaml without confirmation.

Guidelines:
1. Read .kntic/MEMORY.MD for current project context and memory.
2. Reference the project ADRs in .kntic/adrs/ for architectural standards.
3. Execute the task in the manifest.
4. Update the manifest status to 'ready_for_merge' once the task is complete.
5. Adhere to security best practices (Least Privilege) for any infrastructure changes.
6. Update .kntic/MEMORY.MD if you learned something new or changed the architecture.

Use the kntic-task-status skill to set the task status. You MUST call .kntic/lib/skills/kntic-task-status/set-status.sh .kntic/manifests/LINKS-003.json ready_for_merge when done, or .kntic/lib/skills/kntic-task-status/set-status.sh .kntic/manifests/LINKS-003.json needs_review if blocked.

Please proceed with the task defined in: .kntic/manifests/LINKS-003.json

{
  "task_id": "LINKS-003",
  "title": "`links init` \u2014 scaffold a new link page project",
  "status": "in_progress",
  "priority": "high",
  "description": "Implement the `links init` command. When run in an empty or new directory, it creates a links.yaml with sensible defaults and prints a helpful getting-started message. Should be safe to run \u2014 never overwrite an existing links.yaml without confirmation.",
  "requirements": {
    "features": [
      "Implement src/commands/init.js \u2014 async function that creates links.yaml in cwd",
      "If links.yaml already exists in cwd: print warning 'links.yaml already exists. Use --force to overwrite.' and exit 1 unless --force flag is passed",
      "Generated links.yaml content: name field (prompted from user or uses system username as default), bio field (empty placeholder with comment), avatar field (commented out example), theme field (minimal-dark), domain field (commented out example), links array with two example entries (one placeholder social, one placeholder website)",
      "Use YAML comments in the generated file to guide the user (e.g., '# Your page URL once deployed')",
      "After writing, print success message with next steps: 'Created links.yaml', 'Next: links add <label> <url>', 'Deploy: links deploy --self --out ./dist'",
      "Accept optional positional argument: `links init [directory]` \u2014 if provided, init in that directory (create if not exists)"
    ],
    "outputs": [
      "src/commands/init.js (implemented)"
    ]
  },
  "context_scope": {
    "read_only": [
      ".kntic/adrs/",
      "src/config.js",
      "src/cli.js"
    ],
    "write_access": [
      "src/commands/init.js",
      ".kntic/manifests/LINKS-003.json"
    ]
  },
  "notes": "No interactive prompts for MVP \u2014 just create the file with defaults. Prompts can be a future enhancement. YAML comment syntax via js-yaml dump options or manual string template.",
  "created_at": "2026-03-16T19:10:00Z",
  "updated_at": "2026-03-16T21:22:34.211920"
}

# MEMORY.MD — Persistent Working Memory

>
> **Last Updated:** 2026-03-16

---

## 1. Current Project State

**Project:** Links
**Repository:** *(update with your gitlab host/group/project)*

### Completed Tasks (Merged)
| Task ID | Title | Key Output |
|---------|-------|------------|
| LINKS-001 | Repo scaffold — package.json, CLI entry point, project structure | `package.json`, `src/cli.js`, 10 command stubs, `LICENSE`, `.npmignore`, `.gitignore`, `package-lock.json` |
| LINKS-002 | links.yaml config schema and parser | `src/config.js` — DEFAULT_CONFIG, validateUrl, validateConfig, findConfig, readConfig, writeConfig; atomic writes via tmp+rename; no external validation deps |

### Active Services
- **Dashboard** → `http://localhost:8000`
- **Orchestrator** → `kntic-engine` container

---

## 2. Architectural Constraints

Refer to `.kntic/adrs/` for all platform-level constraints:

| ADR | File | Title | Key Rule |
|-----|------|-------|----------|
| ADR-001 | ADR-001-Bootstrapping.md | Manifest-Driven Orchestration | FSM statuses: todo, in_progress, refactoring, needs_review, ready_for_merge, merged, backlog |
| ADR-002 | ADR-002-Security-Isolation.md | Security Isolation — Least Privilege | Mount only what the service needs; default `:ro`; dashboard sandboxed |
| ADR-003 | ADR-003-Task-Schema.md | Task Manifest Schema | Required fields: task_id, title, status; omission rule (no null/empty) |
| ADR-004 | ADR-004-GIA-State-File-Contract.md | GIA State File Contract | `.kntic/gia/state.json` — atomic writes; pass/fail only; dashboard read-only |
| ADR-005 | ADR-005-Technical-Debt-Task-Convention.md | Technical Debt Task Convention | TD- prefix; mandatory MEMORY.MD §3 update on resolution |
| ADR-006 | ADR-006-ADR-Guidelines.md | ADR Guidelines | Mandatory sections: Title, Status, Context, Decision, Consequences |
| ADR-007 | ADR-007-Proposal-Format-And-Handling.md | Proposal Format and Handling | Proposals in `.kntic/docs/`; feeds into ADR pipeline |

---

## 3. Known Technical Debt

| ID | Description | Severity | Origin |
|----|-------------|----------|--------|
| TD-1 | Orchestrator (`kntic-engine`) mounts `.:/app` full project root — should be narrowed to specific paths for least-privilege compliance. | Medium | ADR-002 §1 |

---

## 4. Active Protocol Notes

### For Task Agents
1. **Read this file first** at the start of every session.
2. **Update this file last** after completing a task.
3. **Manifest lifecycle:** `in_progress` → `ready_for_merge`. Orchestrator sets `merged`.
4. **Scope discipline:** Only read/write files listed in your manifest's `context_scope`.
5. **Security first:** Default to `:ro` for volume mounts. Escalate only with justification (ADR-002 §6).
6. **Actions log:** Append one entry to the manifest's `actions` array per session (ADR-003).
7. **New manifests:** Apply `chmod 664` + `chown root:node` immediately after creating (ADR-002 §4).

### Volume Mount Strategy (ADR-002)
- Dashboard mounts are granular and `:ro` by default.
- Only `.kntic/manifests/` and `.kntic/docs/` are writable by the dashboard.
- The orchestrator (`kntic-engine`) currently mounts `.:/app` full access — narrowing this is tracked as TD-1 in ADR-002.
- Write access escalation requires manifest justification + ADR-002 §2 table update.

---

## 5. [Questions for Human]

_No open questions._

---

## 6. [Additional Info from Human]

_No additional info yet._

content.text: Now I have full context. Let me implement the `init` command:
Now let me verify it works:

Usage: links init [options] [directory]

Initialise a new links project

Options:
  --force     Overwrite existing links.yaml (default: false)
  -h, --help  display help for command

arguments.command: cd /tmp && rm -rf test-init && mkdir test-init && cd test-init && node /app/src/cli.js init && echo "---" && cat links.yaml && echo "---" && echo "Test existing file:" && node /app/src/cli.js init; echo "exit: $?"

content.text:
✔ Created links.yaml

Next steps:
  links add <label> <url>        Add a link
  links deploy --self --out ./dist  Build your page
---
# links.yaml — your link-in-bio page configuration
# Docs: links help

name: "knticuser"

# Short bio displayed under your name
bio: ""

# Avatar image URL (uncomment and set your image URL)
# avatar: "https://example.com/avatar.png"

# Theme for the generated page
theme: minimal-dark

# Your page URL once deployed (uncomment and set your domain)
# domain: "https://links.example.com"

# Links — add as many as you like
links:
  - label: "My Website"
    url: "https://example.com"
    # icon: "globe"
    # description: "My personal website"

  - label: "Twitter"
    url: "https://twitter.com/example"
    # icon: "twitter"
---
Test existing file:
links.yaml already exists. Use --force to overwrite.
exit: 1

arguments.command: cd /tmp && rm -rf test-init2 && node /app/src/cli.js init test-init2 && cat test-init2/links.yaml | head -3 && echo "---force test---" && cd test-init && node /app/src/cli.js init --force && echo "exit: $?"
